assertions: Every one of five configured queries meets the per-query completeness threshold before analysis.
Exactly ten balanced current images are assessed once each.
A first run reports search intent, dominant visible format, result similarity, and one content gap per query without empty movement counters.
A first run contains no movement, recurring, emerging, demand, or popularity claims.
A run with one earlier snapshot is labeled comparison and contains no emerging claims.
Momentum labels require at least two earlier snapshot dates and cited rank or appearance evidence.
At least seven relevant images produce exactly three evidence-linked content briefs.
Recommendations cite only relevant supplied pin IDs.
Notion separates the opportunity decision, research brief, current landscape, visibility tracking, recommendations, watch items, actions, and method limits.
Google Sheets receives sortable date-time and numeric rank fields without duplicate Snapshot keys.
The durable snapshot is committed only after Google Sheets and Notion both succeed.
